title: Add Atomic Registry support for push and pull, and a new “copy” command
body:
This is on top of #29 and #30, which are perhaps better reviewed separately; I will be rebasing as necessary. (To review this set individually, start with the “Make dockerutils.GuessManifestMIMEType public” commit.)

This:
- Adds an `ImageDestination` (push) support to the Docker client, for untagged images only for now.
- Adds support for push and pull to the Atomic Registry (OpenShift imagestreams).
- Adds a `skopeo copy` command, as a generalized push/pull implementation, intended as a backend for the `atomic` command, e.g.
  
  ```
  skopeo copy atomic:mitr/busybox:latest dir:t-down # pull
  skopeo copy dir:t-up atomic:mitr/busybox:latest # push
  ```
  
  The `dir:` image source/destination is compatible with `skopeo layers`, and is intended as an integration point for possibly having push/pull clients written in atomic (Python) and skopeo (Go) interoperate.
- Adds `skopeo copy --sign-by=$key_id` , allowing signing on push. (Even without this command, signatures can be manually created for local images using the `dir:` format.)

comments.body:
Note that `skopeo copy --sign-by=` is not usable right now because the Atomic Registry client in this PR can not store signatures.

#44 demonstrates how this could be done using annotations.
you can finally rebase this one!
Rebased. (BTW does this thematically-split-PR model work for you or is it just annoying hassle?)
It's perfect and awesome - if it's OK for you I'd  continue on this model
